skill.name: coding-agent-pm
skill.description: Plan, assign, review, and verify coding-agent work with tight scope control and clear acceptance criteria.
skill.summary: Plan, assign, review, and verify coding-agent work with tight scope control.
skill.author: Bridge AI Labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged backticks, URLs, hidden directory paths, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance keywords. Manual review found these are documentation, installation paths, and prompt templates with no executable code, data access, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting, inline paths, numeric examples, and fenced prompt text. No Ruby code, shell execution, or command runner is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected files contain Markdown prose and fenced text prompts only. No executable Ruby, shell launcher, or command-substitution syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Are Not Network Calls
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged URLs point to the public repository and public download page. They are not used by code and do not transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as plain markdown links in README, listing, and social copy. No fetch, curl, webhook, or credential transfer behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden Directory Paths Are Install Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hidden home-directory paths are common skill installation locations for Claude Code, Codex, and OpenClaw. The files do not read or write those paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are listed under an Install section as destinations for manual copying. There is no script, automation, or filesystem API that accesses them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Keywords Are Natural Language
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static weak-crypto hits come from natural-language words such as description, includes, and destructive. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited line is prose or a checklist item. No MD5, SHA1, DES usage, crypto library import, or hashing behavior appears in context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Are Review Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance-related hits are product copy and review checklist prompts. They do not instruct system probing, port scanning, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines ask humans to verify tests and inspect scope, secrets, and risks. No system commands, network probes, or host enumeration instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Coding agents often drift when briefs, scope, and acceptance criteria are unclear. This skill provides reusable PM templates for assignment, review, verification, and recovery.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured briefs for coding-agent assignments.
Defines scope boundaries, exclusions, and acceptance criteria.
Provides prompt snippets for planning, implementation, review, recovery, and verification.
Supplies a review checklist for behavior, scope, quality, verification, and privacy.
Tracks first-week agent workflow results across five assisted coding tasks.
limitations: It provides templates and guidance, not automated project management software.
It does not run tests, inspect repositories, or enforce file boundaries by itself.
It cannot guarantee agent output quality without human review.
It is focused on coding-agent workflows, not general product discovery.

use_cases.title: Prepare a bounded coding task
use_cases.description: Create a clear agent assignment with owned files, excluded files, acceptance criteria, and required checks.

use_cases.title: Review agent implementation work
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to catch scope drift, regressions, weak tests, and privacy issues before accepting changes.

use_cases.title: Measure early agent workflow quality
use_cases.description: Track time saved, rework, test results, and ship decisions across the first five agent-assisted tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Start With a Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn my feature request into a scoped coding-agent brief. Include outcome, owned files, excluded files, acceptance criteria, and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Assign an Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an agent assignment for this implementation. Keep the scope narrow, require existing project patterns, forbid unrelated changes, and specify the final verification report.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Finished Diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the review checklist to evaluate this completed agent work. Put findings first and focus on regressions, scope drift, missing tests, and privacy risk.

prompt_templates.title: Recover From Scope Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: The agent expanded beyond the assignment. Use the recovery prompts to map each change to acceptance criteria, identify unnecessary edits, and define the next safe step.

output_examples.input: A founder wants an agent to add a checkout draft flow without publishing products.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a brief with the shipped outcome, files in scope, destructive actions forbidden, acceptance criteria, and validation commands.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead needs to accept or reject an agent-generated pull request.
output: Privacy and destructive-command risks are checked.

output_examples.input: A team wants to learn whether coding agents save time during the first week.
output_examples.output: The workflow tracker records five tasks with time saved, rework needed, test status, ship decision, and notes.

best_practices: Define owned files and excluded files before implementation starts.
Require validation commands or manual checks in every assignment.
Review the final diff before accepting agent work.
anti_patterns: Give a vague request without acceptance criteria.
Allow broad refactors during a small feature or bug task.
Accept claimed verification without commands, results, or manual evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill write code for me?
faq.answer: No. It helps you brief, manage, review, and verify coding-agent work.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is designed for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and similar coding agents.

faq.question: Can it prevent all agent mistakes?
faq.answer: No. It reduces ambiguity and improves review, but humans still own the ship decision.

faq.question: Is it useful for nontechnical PMs?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates use plain language for scope, acceptance criteria, validation, and review.

faq.question: Does it require project setup?
faq.answer: No. You can copy the skill and templates into a supported agent skills folder.

faq.question: When should I use the recovery prompts?
faq.answer: Use them when an agent expands scope, changes unrelated files, or stops following acceptance criteria.
